sections.name: Mixed Martial Artist
sections.text: These fighters combine the takedowns of a wrestler with aggressive stances and unorthodox blows, ensuring that contenders won't know what hit them.

traits.name: Human
traits.text: A creature with this trait is a member of the human ancestry. Humans are a diverse array of people known for their adaptability. An ability with this trait can be used or selected only by humans.

traits.name: Humanoid
traits.text: Humanoid creatures reason and act much like humans. They typically stand upright and have two arms and two legs.

family.name: Martial Artist

subtypes.name: Alternate Moves
subtypes.text:
Martial artists rarely have the same repertoire. For variety, use the following table to tweak an NPC's stats: 

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **NPC** | **Ability** | **Replace With** |
| Martial Student |  Fancy Footwork |  Hi-yah! |
| Tournament Combatant |  Work the Crowd  | Competitive Taunt |
| Mixed Martial Artist  | Any Stance Shift option  | Constant Feint Stance |
| Black Belt |  Blocking Counterattack  | Defensive Side Kick |
| Grandmaster |  One-Millimeter Punch | Blazing Gate |

abilities.name: Blazing Gate
abilities.text: [two-actions]  (electricity)

abilities.name: Requirements
abilities.text: The grandmaster has a target grappled or restrained;

abilities.name: Effect
abilities.text: The grandmaster tosses their target upwards and electrocutes them with a qi-infused double palm strike as they land. The target becomes prone, loses the grappled or restrained condition, and takes 5d6 bludgeoning damage and 2d8 persistent electricity damage (DC 38 basic Fortitude save).

abilities.name: Competitive Taunt
abilities.text: [one-action]  (emotion, fear, mental) The tournament combatant attempts to Demoralize a target. On a success, the demoralized creature is off-guard until the end of their next turn. The tournament combatant gets a +2 circumstance bonus to their damage against a creature made off-guard in this way.

abilities.name: Constant Feint Stance
abilities.text: This stance option replaces a different Stance Shift option (usually Secure Grapple Stance). +2 to attempts to Feint. If the mixed martial artist rolls a critical failure to Feint, they get a failure instead. If they successfully Feint, they can use Stance Shift as a free action. Unlike other stances, in this stance the mixed martial artist can't make Strikes.

abilities.name: Defensive Side Kick
abilities.text: [reaction]

abilities.name: Trigger
abilities.text: A creature within the black belt's reach uses a move action;

abilities.name: Effect
abilities.text: The black belt makes a fist Strike designed to intercept and create distance. This Strike does not count towards the black belt's multiple attack penalty, and the black belt's multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this Strike. On a successful hit, the target is pushed 5 feet away.

abilities.name: Kiai!
abilities.text: [free-action]

abilities.name: Frequency
abilities.text: once per turn;

abilities.name: Requirements
abilities.text: The martial student attempts a Strike;

abilities.name: Effect
abilities.text: The martial student shouts to startle their adversary. If the Strike is successful, the target becomes frightened 1.

subtypes.name: Dirty Fighting
subtypes.text: Sometimes it doesn't pay to fight fair. You can give one of these dirty fighting tactics to any martial artist NPC. While tournament combatants are especially likely to look for an edge that will let them win, most black belts and grandmasters are unlikely to stoop to such chicanery.

abilities.name: Concealed Spikes
abilities.text: [reaction]

abilities.name: Trigger
abilities.text: The martial artist critically succeeds on an unarmed Strike;

abilities.name: Effect
abilities.text: Tiny spikes in the martial artist's gloves or footwear deal an additional 1d6 persistent bleed damage.

abilities.name: Contraband Substance
abilities.text: [one-action]  The martial artist pulls out a concealed alchemical vial of mutagen and drinks it to improve their combat prowess. The item is usually a juggernaut mutagen, or quicksilver mutagen. This ability can't be used again until the martial artist has time to acquire and hide a new mutagen. Some martial artists use concealed small vials, but others hide a syringe or soak the alchemical liquid into a garment they can put in their mouth.

abilities.name: Illegal Bite
abilities.text: [one-action]  The martial artist makes an Athletics check against a target's Fortitude DC to bite them in a vulnerable area. On a success, the martial artist deals 2d6 piercing damage (or double on a critical success).

abilities.name: Sand In Your Eye
abilities.text: [one-action]  (visual) The martial artist Feints. On a success, the martial artist tosses sand into the face of one target, blinding them for 1 round or until the target Interacts to remove the blinded condition.

subtypes.name: Grandmaster Forbidden Knowledge
subtypes.text: Grandmasters study techniques outlawed for their tendency to maim both the user and the victim. If the Forbidden Palm doesn't suit your grandmaster's style, swap it for one of the following moves—if you dare.

abilities.name: Life Burn Aura
abilities.text: [three-actions]  (aura, force) The grandmaster transmutes their lifespan into an expulsion of qi. For the next minute, the grandmaster is surrounded by a 30-foot emanation that deals 3d6 force damage to all creatures who start their turn within the emanation, including the grandmaster themself. While Life Burn Aura is active, the grandmaster's fist Strikes deal 2 additional die of weapon damage. When the aura disappears, the grandmaster is permanently doomed 1. This condition is cumulative.

abilities.name: Solar Surge
abilities.text: [three-actions]  (visual) The grandmaster unleashes a qi wave as bright as the sun. All creatures within a 50-foot radius of the grandmaster who can see must attempt a DC 38 Reflex save, including the grandmaster themself.

abilities.name: Critical Success
abilities.text: The creature is dazzled for 1 round.

abilities.name: Success
abilities.text: The creature is blinded for 1 round.

abilities.name: Failure
abilities.text: The creature is blinded for 1 day.

abilities.name: Critical Failure
abilities.text: The creature is permanently blinded.

abilities.name: Super Qi Blast
abilities.text: [one-action] (force, spellshape, polymorph); The grandmaster's body expands as it prepares to exude an unfathomable amount of qi. If the grandmaster's next action is to cast the two-action version of *qi blast* , the size of the cone increases to 120 feet and the damage increases to 16d6. The grandmaster also becomes one size category larger for the next minute, dealing an additional +9 damage with their melee Strikes. Additionally, the grandmaster is permanently drained 1. This drained condition is cumulative.

family.text: Martial artists strive to master the art of hand-to-hand fighting.

traits.name: Agile
traits.text: The multiple attack penalty you take with this weapon on the second attack on your turn is –4 instead of –5, and –8 instead of –10 on the third and subsequent attacks in the turn.

traits.name: Magical
traits.text: Something with the magical trait is imbued with magical energies not tied to a specific tradition of magic. Some items or effects are closely tied to a particular tradition of magic. In these cases, the item has the arcane, divine, occult, or primal trait instead of the magical trait. Any of these traits indicate that the item is magical.

traits.name: Nonlethal
traits.text: Attacks with this weapon are nonlethal, and are used to knock creatures unconscious instead of kill them. You can use a nonlethal weapon to make a lethal attack with a –2 circumstance penalty.

traits.name: Unarmed
traits.text: An unarmed attack uses your body rather than a manufactured weapon. An unarmed attack isn’t a weapon, though has a weapon group and might have weapon traits. An unarmed attack can’t be Disarmed. It also doesn’t take up a hand, though a fist or other grasping appendage generally works like a free-hand weapon.

ability.name: Powerful Fists
ability.text: The martial artist's fist Strikes don't take penalties when making lethal attacks.

ability.name: Stance Shift

traits.name: Stance
traits.text: A stance is a general combat strategy that you enter by using an action with the stance trait, and you remain in for some time. A stance lasts until you get knocked out, until its requirements (if any) are violated, until the encounter ends, or until you use a stance action again, whichever comes first. After you take an action with the stance trait, you can’t take another one for 1 round. You can enter or be in a stance only in encounter mode. You can Dismiss a stance.

ability.text:
The mixed martial artist enters a stance of their choice, gaining the listed circumstance bonus and Strike but losing the ability to make other Strikes. 
* **Elbow Knockout Stance** +2 to Athletics checks to Shove or Trip; **Melee** [#] cross elbow +16 (nonlethal, trip, unarmed) **Damage** 2d6+8 bludgeoning
* **Secure Grapple Stance** +2 to Athletics checks to Grapple; **Melee** [#] grappling limb + 16 (grapple, nonlethal, unarmed) **Damage** 2d4+8 bludgeoning
* **Thrashing Barrage Stance** +2 to damage against off-guard opponents; **Melee** [#] thrashing fist + 16 (forceful, nonlethal, unarmed) **Damage** 2d8+8 bludgeoning

ability.name: Takedown Fluidity
ability.trigger: The mixed martial artist's last action was a successful Grapple, Shove, or Trip.
ability.effect: The mixed martial artist uses Stance Shift.
